description: Record type-name references as consumer deps -- the second half of noting. Walk every type-position in the indexed surface: @spec (params, result, constraint bounds), inline parameter annotations (fn f(x :: T, ...)), extern signatures, and @type bodies. Parse each with the in-house type-expr (.11) and classify each Name against the namespace captured at that declaration point: a Type binding (or a dotted module-path leaf) -> reference_type -> the TypeName identity, recorded as an upstream dep of the consumer it feeds (the FunctionId whose contract/dispatch names it, or the TypeName whose @type body names it). The walk recurses into type arguments, so a name nested in a parametric application (tkf_box(tkf_target), list(T)) is found; arity is part of the identity. An unbound lowercase name is a free type var (not referenced); a builtin is a scalar (not referenced). The recorded set is each consumer wait-set: DeriveTypeDef (.2) reads a type body refs; the contract/dispatch re-seat (.4) reads a function refs. Add world.reference_type and the consumer->[TypeName] dep store. Nothing resolves to Ty; no type-algebra. Verify: a @spec, a @type body (incl. a name nested in a parametric type), and a parameter annotation each record their named types as deps of the right consumer at the right arity; a free type var is not referenced.

comments.text: Covered here: @spec (params, result, constraint bounds), @type bodies, and extern signatures -- the contract-feeding positions the .10 parity gate needs. Body type-test (x is T) references feed body LOWERING, not the contract, so they are recorded in .4 where the body resolver consumes them (re-attribution, not a silent cut).

CORRECTION (supersedes the earlier comment on this issue): there is NO `x is T` construct in fz. What I mislabeled body type-test guards is inline parameter annotations (fn f(x :: T, ...)) -- a FUNCTION type-position, lowered to entry-dispatch type-tests. They are walked HERE in .12 and recorded as function deps, not deferred. No type-position is deferred; .12 covers them all.

title: Reference walk: reference_type over type-positions; record consumer type-name deps
